tests.title: Local Art Exhibition
tests.subtitle: Organisers explain how this art project developed.
tests.text: Local Art Exhibition began when a small group of teenagers became interested in learning more about art. The first meetings were organised in a local youth centre during weekends. [11]At first, organisers believed only a few students would attend regularly[/11]. However, after photos appeared on social media, many more young people became interested in joining. Some activities were difficult to organise because there was little equipment available in the beginning. Despite these problems, participants enjoyed working together and learning practical skills. After several months, experienced members started helping new students during group activities. [12]Organisers discovered that beginners improved faster when older students explained tasks clearly[/12]. Parents and teachers soon noticed that members were becoming more confident and independent. Several participants later decided to continue studying subjects connected with art at college or university. [13]The organisers were surprised that the programme influenced participants’ future plans[/13]. Today, meetings usually include short presentations followed by practical tasks completed in teams. Members are encouraged to ask questions and share ideas openly with the group. Recently, organisers created online videos so students can continue practising at home between sessions. [14]The organisers believe regular practice is more valuable than expecting immediate success[/14]. Although the project is now much larger, organisers still try to create a relaxed and welcoming atmosphere. New members are encouraged to try activities without worrying about mistakes. [15]The main goal is to help young people feel confident trying unfamiliar experiences[/15].

questions.question: What did organisers expect when the project started?

options.B: Students would compete against each other.
options.C: The project would become international immediately.
options.D: Only a small number of students would join.

questions.explanation: Organisers originally expected only a few participants.

questions.question: What helped beginners improve more quickly?
questions.explanation: Older students provided clear explanations and support.

questions.question: Why were organisers surprised later?

options.C: Participants changed their future study plans.

questions.explanation: The programme unexpectedly influenced future studies.

questions.question: What do organisers believe is important?
What is the main aim of the project?

options.B: Helping young people feel confident trying new experiences.

questions.explanation: The programme encourages confidence and participation.
